reviews.review_description:
I have been playing for a while, and I would recommend it. However, there are some things that could make the experience better. 

Same cockpit multiplayer:
I think it would be amazing if you could do free fly or campaign missions in the same cockpit as another player.

Spotify compatibility:
I like to listen to music when I play games on my VR but whenever I try to use Spotify with flight 74 it pauses whatever song I am listening to whenever I hide the menu.

Also, to press buttons and flip switches you have to use the tip of your finger which can be annoying.

The first moment you play ig is ok but then Its so boring the gameplay is like 10 minutes (the story) and then nothing JUST free flight and nothing else the grafficts are so bad and i buyed ts game for 30€ OR 20€ i dont remember and now is 2.99
It’s definitely worth the money! But please add other planes like Boeing and airbus, it would be so good if you added that. And also please add free roam.
Only one plane they should add more planes

SUMMARY:
This review is for the gamers, this is not a game deserving of a 4.1 rating IMO, and my advice is try one of the other  flight games instead that I've listed below, if you haven't already.

DETAILS:
I'm immersing myself in native Quest flight sims at the moment playing in parallel, 'Warplanes: Aircorp (already played the dev's two previous WW1 and WW2 flight games), Dawn Of Jets, and replaying Ultra Wings 2. There's a 40% discount RN on Flight 74 so I thought I try something a bit different as it has a drug cartel story infused. While the concept sounds cool, unfortunately the result is dire! I've played the first episode and decided that I need to get a refund after writting this review. Issues (1) I'm not getting the feeling of flight motion. (2) While the environment looks alright at high altitude, it's generally not good at lower altitude. I could even see bushes/trees popup one by one. First time I landed, it was  on the city street and the cars were twice as big as my plane ;-) (3) The voice acting and dialogue is sooooooo cringe. Litterally anyone could perform this (and probably do better!).  And the static picture of the Drug Lord on the plane's console screen while he's talking to the pilot. Oh lordy ;-)

A very high quality flight simulator.

That being said, the learning curve is steep. It helps to know where the landing gear, flaps and throttle are without looking. Flying the missions was as difficult as removing a walrus tooth using only a toothpick and a Q-tip.

I pre-purchased Flight Unlimited. The trailer was spectacular, spackling, profound, visceral and absolutely mind blowing. I'm guessing that similar to all aviation VR apps, planes must be earned playing absolutely fun missions at best or gruelling tedious missions at worst.
I always and often play Dawn of Jets and Warplanes World War One and World War Two which are life-changing experiences. Simple Planes is very good because of the chance to fly using a yoke rather than a sidestick. Ultra wings 2. Very fun.


To play aviation VR games best, for a sidestick experience, place a flashlight or toy kaleidoscope tube through the strap of the right hand controller. Then place the flashlight with the strap of the handgrip flopped around it under your legs and hold it there. Otherwise it is a less than structurally realistic experience for the Curtiss P-40 Warhawk with the shark teeth. It is a majorly airplane of World War Two because of the shark teeth design. 

For a yoke, loop the straps of both handgrips around whatever and place that under the legs and held firmly. And then get a single chopstick holding each end of the chopstick between the middle finger and the ring finger while holding the handgrips. This brings about a more structurally realistic and integral experience for the Boeing 747 and for the Lockheed Hercules C - 130. 

The Hercules C - 130 is quite something. It can hold four Sherman tanks in its hold. It is like the Percheron workhorse of airplane. It's next level. I look forward to flying it and simply can not wait until release day.

Completed Mission 3 on Flight 74 which opens up Free Play. 
Free Play is absolutely wonderful and surpasses all expectations.
I purchase this game and I was done with it within the first 30 minutes, is there only 6 missions?

I have played many flight games and had a great time. This game though is a source of frustration.

Mostly, and this is a dealbreaker, you have to turn on the plane every single time you want to fly. Turning it on wouldn’t be bad if it worked, BUT IT DOESNT. 

“Press five engine switches and two engine buttons”. Ok, sound easy enough, except every single time it’s pure luck if the switches and buttons register you touching them. 

You are supposed to click the controller trigger to straighten the pointer finger, but 95% of the times it the finger bends just before touching the switches/buttons. Not that it matters because whether it is bend or straight, you wont turn on anything.

The few missions I tried I spent way too long flailing my hands and trying to touch that cockpit panel, but it just wouldn’t register 99 out of 100 times.

The last time I spent upwards of 10 minutes going from gentle movements to UFC style knockdowns but the switches wouldn’t move. No restart or exiting the game changed that. Time to uninstall this frustrating game. 

Spend your money on something else. Buying this game is just wasting your money away.

The controls are hard to grab such as the throttle. That’s a nuisance ￼in itself. And if you played Warplanes: Air Corp, you’ll think this game needs more action. The setup is good but it’s boring. I need action. This is my opinion. I’ve played the free fly mode and multiplayer mode too (you can’t find a multiplayer) it’s just not that exciting. This game should be free.

developer_response.body: Sorry you feel this way...thanks for playing and thanks for the comments

reviews.review_description:
It’s impossible to grab the landing gear or flaps in the cockpit like said by other people. 

So this game is a waste of Monney. Should be great of commands could be used easyly. Don’t buy!
solid flight game with realistic controls. wish there were more great flight games like this on the vr. would definitely recommend for someone who's looking for flight experience

Haven't tried multiplayer yet and I'm not really sure how it works for a game like this.  The story line is humorous also. Looking forward to progressing through this!

reviews.review_description:
It’s a brilliant game with an even better storyline, but I think a few things could make it a whole lot better. 

1. Make the plane more interactive:
Give us more buttons to use like lights or the ability to open doors, this would make the game much more realistic and would encourage people to just fly around and would make the game more realistic and enjoyable. 

2. Make the plane feel faster: 
I understand that a twin prop Cessna is quite a slow aircraft in real life, but in the game even if the speedometer says you are flying at 200 knots the plane feels really slow. If you compare your speed to the cars on the ground, you realise how slowly you are going. 

3. Give us more multiplayer options: 
For example, you could allow us to invite a bunch of friends into our plane and be able to have a co pilot with us that can help manage all the systems (<check suggestion 1). And also have the ability to swap out who is flying, so that everyone can have a go. 

4.(This one plays into number 3) Give us more locations to visit:
Give us some nice scenic airports to go land at and explore on foot with friends or alone. This would allow people to go exploring with their friends, land at an airport, open up the door and go have a look around. 


I think these 4 suggestions would take the pressure off of the combat multiplayer modes, which people will eventually get bored of. And instead create more of a sandbox experience where people can create there own stories and make their own fun, which will give you as developers more time to work on high quality updates without worrying about people getting bored of replaying the same combat game modes / storyline etc.

developer_response.body: These are all great ideas! Thanks for taking the time to write this out. We’ll go over these in our next dev meeting!

reviews.review_description: I played the demo and I liked it. However, it gets quite repetitive as you get to do always the same things. I'd like to see more interactions inside the cockpit in order to have a more immersive experience. Sometimes, it gets boring as you just have to fly straight and the scenery-graphics are not nice in order to compensate. The shooting part is fun at first but it is too clunky. You only have 13 missions for the moment and there is nothing really happening. The multiplayer is not that interesting either (I don't get why we have to restart a new game and search for new players after the 3 kills). All in all, I am happy I didn't pay full price.

developer_response.body: The game starts out slowly to teach unseasoned pilots how to fly, and get the hang of the controls.  The game adds strategic elements in the later missions.

reviews.review_title: Soar like an eagle...in the mob
reviews.review_description: I didn't know what to expect but I'm pleasantly surprised! Not many games really feel like you're there, but I really felt like I was flying.  I almost punched a hole through my wall when I had to hit the cockpit window, that says it all.  I'll have fun with this one for time to come.  The multiplayer looks cool also but I didn't get a chance to jump into a game yet.  Expect a review update once I do.  I give this one 2 thumbs up and 5 stars!

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing, we appreciate your support!

reviews.review_description:

Is it possible to clear mission 13? I don't know what to do.
I would like to fly with Google Earth in the background.

reviews.review_description:
Flight 74 offers an engaging concept with a solid storyline, making it enjoyable for aviation enthusiasts. The immersive startup sequence, where you flip switches and prepare for takeoff, adds a great level of realism. Controls feel intuitive, and small details—like grabbing a cup of coffee or dealing with a stray pigeon—bring charm to the experience. The short missions make it easy to hop in and play without a big time commitment.

Visually, while the game isn’t the most detailed, it still delivers a convincing and immersive atmosphere. There are occasional graphical glitches, and some environments feel repetitive, but these don’t take away from the core experience.

Overall, Flight 74 is a fun and immersive VR flight experience that captures the excitement of piloting a plane and doing missions, with room for refinement in visual variety.
Crappy

developer_response.body: We’re sorry you didn’t enjoy the game. New updates coming soon

reviews.review_description: One of the best stabdalone flight simulator for quest 2/3! I wish the auto pilot would work for free flight mode, new maps and bump the graphics a bit and this game is gold! Cant wait for the new campaign!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support. We have a big multiplayer update coming soon!

reviews.review_description: Disappointing for me.

developer_response.body: We’re sorry you didn’t enjoy the game. We have new updates scheduled to release in a few weeks

reviews.review_description:
Flight 74 takes the thrill of flight simulation and mixes it with the chaotic charm of an arcade game, delivering a hilariously unique experience. Set in a world where you’re a pilot for a not-so-secret cartel, the game blends high-stakes missions with laugh-out-loud moments.

The controls strike a balance between realism and accessibility, making it enjoyable for both flight sim enthusiasts and casual gamers. 

The humor is top-notch, with over-the-top dialogue and ridiculous mission objectives. The graphics are on par for a standalone VR game.

While it might not have the depth of a hardcore flight sim, Flight 74 more than makes up for it with its charm and creativity. It’s a wild, hilarious ride that’s perfect for anyone looking to take to the skies with a twist.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to write your review and comments, we're updating frequently, check back for significant multiplayer improvements this week!

reviews.review_description:
Storyline isn't so bad, if the controls and VR flight were more realistic and responsive like the Warplanes series of WW1, WW2 Pacific & Modern; it would gain more than 1 ⭐.

Waste of money & time if you ask me, which you didn't 😆 

If you want a fun & more realistic flight ✈️ game, try the Warplanes series...

developer_response.body: Sorry you aren't enjoying the game.  The flight mechanics of a Cessna are much different than those of WW1 & WW2 combat planes.  We're working on additional updates, please check back in a week or two

Can anyone give me the secret of avoiding the missle strike in mission 13. The coffee clue I’m assuming is to direct you to the gernade. Ive tried following the screen circle but no bueno. After 2 hours of trying I can’t avoid or stop the missile launch. Appreciate any help.
Problem resolved. Thank you!!

developer_response.body: Did you drink the coffee?  When you drink it, there's a code on the bottom of the cup that'll shut down the missile launch

reviews.review_description:
full disclaimer: I was recruited on reddit by the developer and given the game for free to test the first 3 missions and give feedback, the developer also asked me to write a review, and they basically said to either leave a 5 star review or to not leave any review at all. with that said I do believe this game deserves 5 stars for several reasons.


It's obviously not microsoft flight sim, you have to remember this is running on a standalone system that is basically a phone. 


there are some bugs and graphical glitches in the game, but the developer is active in trying to fix them and make the game better, unlike many other games on this platform that are just straight up abandonware.


For what it is, a $10 game, I've put about 3 hours into it (I'm only on mission 8 so far) and had quite a bit of fun with it. I found the first mission a little bit frustrating but after setting it down for a bit and giving it another go the other missions were quite enjoyable after I was used to how to fly the plane.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your comments and helpful testing.  We have some major updates in the works that will release in the next few weeks.

reviews.review_description: It took forever and a day to get past the first level. It was all too stressful. This really isn't my kind of game. I'm not a pilot, the controls are either too sensitive or unresponsive. I'm focused on all the wrong details and I'm easily confused, I'm a slow learner who's always a step behind and I find playing the game frustrating.

developer_response.body: Sorry the game was too much of a challenge for you.  The first mission isn't designed to be overly difficult.  We designed the first few missions to explain the game while providing a small challenge.

reviews.review_description:
I’ve been longing for a flight game for a while. I have in the past been streaming PCVR to my headset in order to play well known flight sims. But it’s not convenient when you want to just want to throw on the headset and jump into something. 

Although it does have some flight sim similarities it’s more of an arcade flight game. But in reality the devs have done a fantastic job of making it feel like and the plane handle like you are actually controlling one.  If your like me sometimes I want just to jump in and take to the sky to glance out the windows and look down on the scenery… 

The graphics are probably the best if you’re comparing to all the other flight games on the Meta store. For now there is just one plane you can fly, hopefully the developers will add additional planes to the game in the future.

The story wasn’t what I was buying it for, I just wanted to scratch that itch of flying. The free flight mode only opens up after completion of the 3rd mission of the story mode. Which at first was bugged me because I just wanted to fly. However the missions are not that difficult and you progress fairly quickly though the first 3 which will then open the free flight mode.

Overall a great title, good graphics and has a lot of potential for the future if development continues. I personally think this game has huge potential to become something spectacularly with some updates and add ons.

What I would love to see more in game.

Additional planes
Free flight unlocked from the beginning
Better Analog controller support, not just grip and hold the flight controls. “But does give a sense of realism” ☺️
More locations, real world
Mods.

Thanks Devs for an awesome game

developer_response.body: Thanks for the long review and helpful comments.  Great ideas for updates...we're working hard on the multiplayer update (2 weeks away), after that we'll start additional improvements

reviews.review_description:
Really ugly on Q3, the developers should maybe learn about optimization techniques, the Q3 deserves better and can do WAY better. Looks like a PS1 game.

Developers : trees looks like just 2D sprites, and even if they were 3D, there's not so much polygons here. The size of the terrain shouldn't be a big deal if you were lazy loading only a restricted area around the plane position (no need to load the entire map in memory). That way you could increase the polygons a LOT. Did you looked at Red Matter 2 ? Those guys really pushed the hardware, and they're not complaining about the size of the map, the amount of polygons displayed in Flight 74 is just ridiculous compared to Red Matter 2. I'm not even talking about the textures and shaders.

Flight 74 goes regularly on sale for as low as 2.00$ (on Reddit), which says quite a lot. 

That game sounds like a hobby/side project from an indie developer and while it certainly does represent a lot of work for a single person, it's clearly overpriced and/or need to deliver more on such hardware. Optimization is the key.

developer_response.body:
We have very complex algorithms for optimization.  I don't think you realize how difficult it is to build a flight game on mobile hardware.  We experimented with 3D trees but we can increase the tree density 3x by using photo realistic 2D, with various blending techniques.  After months of development time on trees alone, we concluded 2D with our blending approach is the way to go.  The Quest hardware requires constant calculations for distant objects, close objects, etc.  (Red Matter doesn't have this problem).  We've developed our own proprietary methods and algorithms that push the rendering to the max on the Quest.

We're aware of Red Matter 2.  You aren't understanding the challenge with Flight Sim environments. Large environmental draw distances are a major challenge with the hardware.  Small enclosed environments don't require rendering in the distance and are a different animal...if we had small enclosed spaces similar to Red Matter or other FPS games, we could boost the graphics significantly, but that isn't the case.  We use complicated algorithms that present objects in specific ways in the distance.   If you saw what's happening under the hood, I think you would be impressed with how it works.

As for the sales, it's very difficult to promote App Lab titles, so we have to get creative.  It's not a reflection of the game quality.  It's a reflection of our limitations to market App Lab titles.  

We're working on more updates though, so stay tuned.  We're working tirelessly to improve every aspect of the game.

reviews.review_description: I find this game quite enjoyable, although the missions are not particularly easy, which is a positive aspect. However, it would be advantageous to make improvements in the graphical department. Some elements lack detail during missions, whether it's distant planes or even landscapes. Such an enhancement would be especially appreciated, especially for those playing with a latest-generation VR headset.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing, we're using advanced algorithms to adjust draw distances during gameplay.  We're pushing the Quest 3 pretty hard and have to make trade offs between tree densities, foliage densities, draw distances, etc.  We'll continue to optimize in updates.

reviews.review_description: This is a very good campaign based flight simulator. The graffics are nice and the gameplay is flawless. The campaign is cool and make the start in this game very easy without a traditional tutorial feeling. Normally I have some problems with motion sickness but not here. This game is a must have for simulator fans. Good work guys hope for some new stuff in coming updates.

developer_response.body: We appreciate your support and feedback, thank you

reviews.review_description: Flight 74 soars beyond the typical flight simulation, offering an adventurous escapade that tickles the funny bone. Its gameplay seamlessly blends thrilling aerial maneuvers with a storyline brimming with good humor. The controls are intuitive, making each flight a joy rather than a chore.

developer_response.body: We're thrilled you like the game, if you have any wish list items please email mail@tasappsystems.com

I couldn't see the arm of my actual chair but I had to reach over it to grab them 😂😂 Def 5 atars

Pretty funny and well designed… worth the price and might be evolutionary

reviews.review_description: A must buy

reviews.review_description: Great game

developer_response.body: This isn't a fault with the game, it sounds like a problem with the Quest store.  Please email us so we can help, these 1 star reviews hurt us.  mail@tasappsystems.com

reviews.review_description: This game is so fun i love it

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, we're happy you're enjoying Flight 74

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I wanted. Give it a shot!
reviews.review_description: Totally honest review here. I was looking for a flight / flying game for my quest and I've been looking at a few titles for a while now. Nothing that made me make the purchase so far. I go through sidequest and applab listing quite often and this one must have gotten right past me or something. What drew me in today was the art, the logo and it was a flight game I was wanting after all. STILL unsure I watched some videos including the trailer which really doesn't do it justice because I went ahead and took advantage of the half off sale they have going, and personally I got a game that's worth more than 9.99 to me. I have paid the same or more for much less. It's a flight game again like I was looking for but the spin they have on it is cool, the whole kidnapped by cartel feel to it is fun, and the visuals are great on quest 2. I like the water, and the mountains look good too. The city didn't look bad either, The prison or military base looks similar to an early GTA building I found that pretty neat. As far as what I feel could use some work? Maybe on collisions / impacts? An explosion when you impact the ground or mountain would be great, ESPECIALLY when you spill the coffee ( I was really anticipating that.) Overall though it's cool I'm not even finished with the whole thing but we're in a shady situation, pigeons are breaking through the glass and you got to swat them out. Drink coffee for stamina, and shooting portions. WORTH IT. If there continues to be updates like the developers are saying in other comments and have been doing, It can only get better. Thanks guys glad I came across your game and I will be doing a review on my YouTube channel! 🤘🙏

developer_response.body: Thanks for your comments, we have many updates in the works and we'll take your comments into consideration.  If you have any ideas or specifics you'd like us to change (other than what's in your review) please send us an email.  mail@tasappsystems.com

reviews.review_title: Great Game, waiting for Quest 3 upgrade!
reviews.review_description: very good game!  I would love to see the resolution and quality of the plane's textures improve, I think that with Quest 3 it can improve a lot and be one of the best simulators, it would also be nice if it added other planes like the Cesna but I understand that it is difficult to create something like that.

developer_response.body: A Quest 3 upgrade is coming, we're hoping to get into the official App Store which will allow us to develop significant updates.

reviews.review_description: So I bought this game 2 weeks ago and since then I’ve been playing making the most of my experience and played around in the game modes and everything there’s not 1 single thing that’s ruined my experience I recommend this game for people who like the sound of a fully interactive cockpit and flying controlling the plane is easy and my overall rating 10/10

developer_response.body: Please check out the latest update with Hand Tracking and Quest 3 specific graphics udpates

reviews.review_description:
I’ve only played the first 3-4 missions, but I can say right off the bat, these are the best controls of any of the flying games I’ve played. Cockpit graphics are pretty good, as well as a quite beautiful menu area (this is also where you’ll get some live news updates from the story). 

Hand tracking as well works incredibly well. I did switch back to controllers for a bombing mission as I apparently dropped the bomb without realizing it so I’d recommend a controls overview screen somewhere in the main menu. 

Overall it definitely doesn’t feel like an applab game! Definitely recommend. 

Gameplay wise it’s a bit slower than say, warplanes, as you are flying a cessna and there isn’t (so far, at least) any air-air combat since well, you don’t have any guns (but one of the first 3 missions is a proper bombing run where you actually have to aim your drop - big change from the typical “just drop it in this 5 mile radius”). But I’m only a few missions in so this may change - there’s also a free flight mode that has probably 10 or so different airports to take off from and I’ve only unlocked one, so there very well may be combat later in the game since I’m obviously pretty early on still. 

There’s also a big emphasis on landing/takeoff (along with fully interactable switches and buttons for preflight) which warplanes mostly shoves under the rug. 

Having a ton of fun! Time to get back in the air :)

developer_response.body: We really appreciate the well thought out comments and feedback.  Especially excited you enjoy the hand tracking
